config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because individual rights are what protect people from abuse by the majority or the state. In my view, society works best when it respects personal freedom while still setting fair limits for safety and public order. From my perspective, collective interests matter, but they should not be used as an excuse to silence or sacrifice individuals too easily.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, rights like free speech, due process, and privacy are exactly what keep majorities and governments from steamrolling unpopular people, and history shows that “for the good of society” can become a dangerous excuse. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, because public safety and basic order matter, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully. In other words, society matters, but it should be constrained by strong individual rights, not placed above them by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. From my perspective in Indonesia, society matters a lot, but I do not accept the idea that collective interests should automatically outrank individual rights. We have seen how appeals to public order or harmony can be used to pressure people into silence, and that is dangerous because it protects power more than it protects the public. I do think rights can be limited in narrow cases for safety and social stability, but the default should still be that the individual is protected, not sacrificed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual by default. From my American perspective, that idea is exactly how governments and majorities end up justifying censorship, discrimination, and abuse while calling it the common good. I do think there are real cases where individual rights need limited constraints for public safety, but those limits have to be narrow, transparent, and heavily justified. If society is allowed to override rights too easily, then the word "society" just becomes a polite label for whoever has the most power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, society is important, but I do not accept the idea that it should automatically come before the rights of the individual. In practice, appeals to harmony, order, or the common good can easily be used to pressure people into silence, and that often protects authority more than it protects ordinary people. Individual rights should be the default, with limits only in narrow cases where safety and public order are genuinely at risk.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, that framing is too dangerous because it gives governments and majorities an easy way to excuse censorship, discrimination, and abuse in the name of the common good. Society does matter, but it is strongest when it is built around protected rights, not when rights are treated as disposable whenever they become inconvenient. I can accept narrow limits for real public safety needs, but the burden has to stay on society to prove those limits are necessary and tightly controlled.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
